title: 1.1: Reports the log messages that appear when a ramping executor with at least 5 VUs receives a SIGINT (e.g.: "Stopping k6 in response to signal...", "Metrics emission of VUs and VUsMax metrics stopped" etc).

1.2: States that VUs mid-iteration are allowed to finish their current iteration before being terminated during SIGINT shutdown (e.g.: concludes that k6 performs graceful shutdown based on log evidence).

1.3: Reports the gRPC server streaming test log entries when using a 30ms graceful ramp down (e.g.: "Regular duration is done, waiting for iterations to gracefully finish", "Graceful stop", "stream is cancelled/finished" etc).
1.4: States the value of the number of gRPC messages received (e.g.: 500) from the metrics summary.
1.5: States the number of dropped iterations for a test that exceeds its maximum duration capacity, obtained by querying the API (e.g.: 153).
1.6: Shows test output to demonstrate that there is a memory leak where memory usage grows linearly with VU count (e.g.: 1 VU: " (kbytes): 606316", 10 VU: "(kbytes): 1727628" etc).

1.7: Identifies the root cause of the memory footprint behavior in the CSV module (e.g.: module.go using time.Now().Nanosecond() to generate unique SharedArray names, preventing data sharing across VUs).
